page: Amazon Web Services is4gen.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. Built on the 64-bit ARM architecture (arm64), this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with single-thread-per-core execution. The hardware configuration includes 96 GB of system memory, providing a 1:6 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. A key feature of this instance is its bundled local storage, consisting of 15,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across most multi-core and database workloads. The instance achieved average ratings in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core testing. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 946.86 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 73.29 GB/sec. However, single-threaded performance is a notable tradeoff, with weak ratings in Geekbench single-core and poor ratings in single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Database operations and Redis SET operations both perform in the average tier, with Redis handling 19.11 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the is4gen.4xlarge offers high storage density per resource unit, making it a viable option for workloads requiring massive local storage without the premium of high-frequency x86 processors. The instance is optimized for storage-intensive and database workloads, such as distributed file systems, transactional databases, and large-scale caching layers. It is less suited for single-threaded compute tasks or large language model inference, where it exhibits weak to poor performance.
description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 96 GB of system memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. The defining hardware feature is its bundled 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering high storage density. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU and database performance, with the instance achieving 19.11 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and average marks in Passmark database operations. However, single-threaded performance is a tradeoff, scoring weakly in Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tests. This instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy deployments by pairing dense local NVMe storage with energy-efficient ARM architecture. It is best suited for transactional databases, caching layers, and distributed file systems.
og_description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.4xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring 16 Graviton cores, 96 GB of RAM, and 15 TB of local NVMe SSD storage, ideal for database and high-density storage workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.4xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance with 16 Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 15 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring fifteen terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage and ninety-six gigabytes of system memory.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 15,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Features 96 GB of system memory and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers average multi-core and database performance with 19.11 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for storage-intensive workloads, transactional databases, and caching layers
